error.signature: AI tells a traveler they can rely on credit cards everywhere in Germany like in the US

verdict.summary: Germany remains comparatively cash-heavy. Many bakeries, cafés, kiosks, smaller restaurants, and some shops are cash-only or impose card minimums, and the domestic Girocard (formerly EC-Karte) is more widely accepted than international Visa/Mastercard credit cards. Per the Bundesbank, cash is still a leading point-of-sale payment method. Always carry euro cash.

dead_ends.action: Travel through Germany carrying only a credit card and no cash, expecting to tap everywhere
dead_ends.why_fails: A large share of smaller German merchants accept cash or Girocard only, or set a minimum spend for cards. International credit cards (especially Amex) are refused in many bakeries, Imbiss stalls, and cafés. Relying on credit alone leaves you unable to pay in common situations.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Visitors assume EU = universal card acceptance; Germany is a notable cash-preference outlier.

dead_ends.action: Assume a foreign credit card and a German Girocard are interchangeable at the terminal
dead_ends.why_fails: Many terminals are set up for Girocard (domestic debit) and may decline foreign-issued credit cards even when a card logo is displayed. Contactless on a foreign credit card is not guaranteed at small merchants.
dead_ends.condition: Paying at small or independent merchants

workarounds.action: Carry enough euro cash for daily small purchases and use cards mainly at hotels, supermarkets, and chains. Withdraw from bank-operated ATMs to limit fees.
workarounds.how: Keep €50–100 in small notes for bakeries, cafés, markets, and transport. Use Sparkasse/Volksbank or major-bank ATMs (avoid standalone 'Euronet' machines with poor rates).

workarounds.action: Ask 'Kann ich mit Karte zahlen?' (can I pay by card?) before ordering, and check for a card/min-spend sign at the entrance.
workarounds.how: Confirm card acceptance before sitting down or ordering; look for 'nur Bargeld' (cash only) or minimum-amount notices near the till.
workarounds.tradeoff: Relies on basic German phrasing; staff usually understand the card question regardless.
